steps.title: Find the clause boundary
steps.content: The sentence first states that the sand at Les Sables Roses Beach is an unusual shade. It then gives the contrasting explanation: crushed coral and other organic matter make the sand pink.

steps.title: Punctuate the transition
steps.content: Choice B and Choice C do not correctly separate the transitional word from the next independent clause, and Choice D puts the semicolon before the transition. The pattern shade, though; deposits correctly joins the clauses. The answer is A.
